english: what you say is true, you are right Dzl.; bdág-gis nyés-pa bdén-gyis (bdag gis nyes pa bden gyis) it being true that I committed a fault Dzl.; dé-bzhin-du bden srid (de bzhin du bden srid) it might be true after all Glr.; śin-tu yaṅ bden (shin tu yang bden) to be sure, that is true! Glr.; de bdén-par ṅés-sam (de bden par nges sam) is it quite certain that this is true? Glr.; é'bden ltós-la bdén-par _dúg-na (e 'bden ltos la bden par 'dug na) ... see whether it is true, and if it is, then ... Pth.; bdén-par _dzin-pa (bden par 'dzin pa) to believe to be true, to take for granted bdén-_dzin źig-na (bden 'dzin zhig na) the illusion being destroyed Thgr.; *dén-če-če* (*den ce ce*) W. (for yid-čes-pa (yid ches pa)) to believe, to be persuaded of the truth, frq.; bden bden (bden bden) very true indeed! certainly; bden-bdén-ma (bden bden ma) prob. something in which there is much truth Tar. — 2. to be in the right, to be right, kyed bod-blon-rnams bden ye (khyed bod blon rnams bden ye) Tibetan ambassadors are in your full right Glr.; *ňa á-sál-la dén-pa soň* (*nga a sal la den pa song*) W. I have evidently been right. II. sbst. 1. truth, in the abstract; but usually: something true, true words etc., bdén-pa smra-ba (bden pa smra ba) to tell or speak the truth; as adj.: true, veracious Stg., (W. *dén-pa zér-kan* (*den pa zer mkhan*)); mi-bden-rdzún (mi bden rdzun) this is not truth but falsehood Glr.; bden-pa mtóṅ-ba (bden pa mthong ba) to discern, to know, the truth, a degree of Buddhist perfection Tar.; bdén-pa bzhi (bden pa bzhi) the four truths, the four realities, viz. pain, the origin of pain, the annihilation of pain, and the way of annihilating it, v. Köpp. I., 220. Whether, when bdén-pa gnyis (bden pa gnyis) are mentioned, they refer to two of the just named realities, or whether they always denote absolute (objective) truth (don-dám-pai bdén-pa (don dam pa'i bden pa)) and subjective truth (kun-rdzób-kyi bdén-pa (kun rdzob kyi bden pa)) as mentioned by Was. (293), I am not prepared to decide, nor am I able to explain the meaning of lám-gyi bdén-pa (lam gyi bden pa) and _góg-pai bdén-pa ('gog pa'i bden pa) (Thgy. frq.). bden-pa-nyíd (bden pa nyid) seems to be a technical term for truth, though the Buddhist understands by it nothing but stoṅ-pa-nyíd (stong pa nyid). Nevertheless, the possibility of its being misapprehended from this reason ought to be no obstacle to the word being used in its original sense, and re-established in its proper right, the more so, as Buddhist philosophy makes but a mockery of truth by identifying it with a negation of reality. — 2. = bden-tsig (bden tshig), v. below, Mil. Comp. *dén-daṅ, dén-da* (*den dang, den da*) W. in truth, certainly. — bden-po (bden po) a true, a just man Cs. — bden-brál (bden bral) Cs.: 1. 'void of truth, unjust. 2. southwest part or direction'. — bden-tsig (bden tshig) 1. a true word Mil., but usually 2. a solemn asseveration, often combined with a prayer, to which the power of securing infallible fulfilment is ascribed Dzl. and elsewh., frq. — bden-_dzin (bden 'dzin) v. above.

= bdé-bar (bde bar); gaṅ-bdér (gang bder) whichever you like, at your pleasure; ci-bdér (ci bder) has a similar meaning. v. Tar. 69, 14, and prob. also 192, 4; bder-bkod (bder bkod) v. _gód-pa ('god pa) bdér-_gro (bder 'gro) v. bdé-ba (bde ba).

1. Cs. 'abundance, exuberance'; more corr., acc. to Zam., where it is explained by dár-ba (dar ba) and तीव्र (unbounded), to extend (intr.) without bounds. — 2. with la, to hurt, to injure a person Dom. and elsewh.; dgra bdó-ba (dgra bdo ba) v. sdaṅ-ba (sdang ba).

I. vb. 1. W. to get or take possession of, to stow away, to house, *ston-tóg* (*ston thog*) the harvest; to put into, *gám-mi náṅ-du* (*gam mi nang du*) something into a box; to lay up or by, to keep, esp. *dóg-te bór-če* (*dog te bor ce*) in store, on hand; *ug náṅ-du dóg-če* (*ug nang du dog ce*) to hold one's breath — 2. B. to be in possession, to be possessed of, gen. with la, like yód-pa (yod pa), dé-la rás-yúg rčig bdog (de la ras yug gcig bdog) he is in possession of only one piece of cloth Dzl.; kyód-la _di-_dra-bai slób-ma bdóg-gam (khyod la 'di 'dra ba'i slob ma bdog gam) have you such scholars? Dzl.; nor mi bdóg-pa (nor mi bdog pa) Dzl. poor; dgón-pa ni gáṅ-na bdog (dgon pa ni gang na bdog) Mil. where have you (where is) your monastery? bdág-la púg-pa bdog (bdag la phug pa bdog) I have a cavern Mil.; in an absolute sense: tabs bdóg-gam mi bdog (thabs bdog gam mi bdog) are there any means or not? Ma.; W. *yin-dog-čan* (*yin dog can*) is stated to mean proud, arrogant; *yóg-dog-čan* (*yog dog can*) one that saves money, a scraper. II. sbst. wealth, riches, B.; cog to bdág-po (cog to bdag po).
pf. of _drál-ba ('dral ba), Dzl. frq. (s. l. c.)
a sort of large unburnt bricks of mud or clay Cs.
